Launch HN: Bloomy (YC S26) – AI-powered mastery learning for K-12
93 points by alexsouthmayd 22 hours ago | hide | past | favorite | 98 comments

Hi HN, I’m Alex Southmayd, the founder of Bloomy (https://bloomylearning.com) – an AI-powered mastery-learning platform for K-12 students. Bloomy provides students with an AI tutor alongside adaptive curriculum (right now Math, English Language Arts, and Writing).

How it works: we diagnose students’ skill gaps, place them on personalized learning paths, and give them standards-aligned lessons and a Socratic AI tutor that scaffolds their learning without just giving away the answer.

The goal is to solve the Bloom 2-sigma problem (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloom%27s_2_sigma_problem) with AI.

Short launch video: https://tinyurl.com/bloomylearning

Longer product demo: https://youtu.be/XHvoKt6qMeo

Families access for Bloomy: https://bloomylearning.com/families

I started as a teacher. I taught 7th-grade English and writing with Teach For America, and every day I struggled to deliver differentiated instruction to 30 students with 30 different sets of needs. Some students needed remediation, some needed acceleration, and many needed a tutor sitting next to them helping them reason through the next step. Benjamin Bloom’s two-sigma result—that one-on-one tutoring can produce much better outcomes than conventional classroom instruction—always felt intuitively true to me. The hard part was making that kind of attention affordable and available to every child.

Then AI changed the cost curve. When I saw schools such as Alpha organize academics around mastery rather than seat time, the model clicked. If you’ve heard of Alpha School, that is directionally the kind of learning model that inspired us. But I kept thinking about the families and schools that already exist: homeschool families, microschools, hybrid schools, and regular classrooms where most children are today.

Most students and teachers see learning gaps at the wrong resolution. They get a grade, percentile, benchmark score, or broad standard—not “this is the next skill this student should learn.” Existing personalized-learning products often feel like digital worksheets: they provide plenty of practice, but not much diagnosis or teaching. Very few have AI tutors providing the core instruction. Bloomy starts with a diagnostic—we integrate with third-party assessments and provide our own—and creates a learning path for each student. Students work one skill at a time, receive a short lesson, practice at an adaptive difficulty, and only move forward after demonstrating at least 90% mastery. The learning path updates as the student works, based on their performance and our knowledge graph of skill prerequisites (built in collaboration with Learning Commons / Chan Zuckerberg Initiative).

Each skill has three stages. Base Camp teaches the concept with worked examples. Climb provides guided practice and Socratic support. Summit is an independent ten-question mastery assessment with no hints or AI assistance. Students need to achieve 90% on the Summit to advance. If they struggle too much, they’ll be routed to a different skill better suited for their level.

BloomyBot is not a blank chat window but rather a live, interactive, and observant digital tutor. During practice, it receives the active passage or problem, the question, the student’s attempt, an authored explanation, and relevant misconception context. It follows a scaffolded tutoring ladder: first asking what the student tried, then pointing toward the concept, suggesting a strategy, working through one step together, and only providing heavier scaffolding after the student has struggled, adapting to and learning from the student along the way. Students can interrupt it, and we’ve begun to roll out multilingual support for Spanish, French, and a few other more niche languages that customers have asked for.

We currently use a variety of Anthropic and OpenAI models for BloomyBot. The tutor is restricted to the current lesson, redirects unrelated questions, limits conversation length, and is unavailable during mastery assessments. The language model does not choose the curriculum or decide whether a student has mastered a skill.

That separation is important. A conventionally “helpful” AI response can be a bad tutoring response: if it gives away the answer, the student completes the task but may not learn anything. Our goal is not to build a homework-answering chatbot. It is to put AI inside a structured loop of diagnosis, instruction, practice, feedback, and independent mastery.

LLMs can still be wrong, and we do not claim our constraints eliminate that. We reduce the surface area by grounding BloomyBot in authored lesson content, keeping it on topic, logging conversations, and removing it from assessments. Teachers and parents can review tutoring activity, students can report problems, and safety signals trigger human alerts and a backup audit. We also do not see Bloomy as a replacement for teachers, parents, or human tutors. A good human tutor is better. The narrower question we are testing is whether, during a bounded learning session a student would already be doing, a context-aware tutor can provide better help than static “correct/incorrect” feedback. Longer term, the question becomes more whether a student would perform better with one-on-one AI tutoring (at least in certain aspects of the curriculum) than with many-to-one instruction in a medium- or large-sized classroom.

Bloomy is now being used across several settings: traditional districts, charter schools, hybrid schools, microschools, homeschools, and families looking for additional academic support. In an early pilot at a charter school in Massachusetts serving ~150 students in grades 6 through 8, students averaged roughly 1.8 times the expected winter-to-spring NWEA MAP growth. This was an observational pilot, not a randomized study, so we treat it as an encouraging signal rather than proof that Bloomy caused the difference.

Parents and teachers can see what a student has mastered, what is in progress, and where support may be needed. We have found that adults generally do not want another generic score; they want to know which small number of skills deserve attention this week.

Bloomy makes money through family subscriptions and school licensing. ELA costs $39/month or $279/year per learner, and Writing Studio costs $19/month or $139/year. Math is scheduled to launch July 31 at the same price as ELA. Schools and microschools pay per student, with pricing varying by subject coverage, enrollment, rostering, and implementation needs.

Because children use Bloomy, we collect learning responses, progress data, and tutoring conversations. We do not sell personal information, use child data for behavioral advertising, or permit model providers to train general-purpose models on identifiable child data sent by Bloomy. We have Zero Data Retention agreements with both Anthropic and OpenAI. Parents and schools can request access, export, correction, or deletion under the applicable account or school agreement.

More background on me: after Teach For America, I taught and designed GMAT and GRE curriculum for Manhattan Prep / Kaplan, led the driver acquisition team for Lyft’s New England markets, completed an MBA at Stanford, and led AI transformation projects at McKinsey (so when models finally became good enough this past January to achieve the kinds of things I am pursuing with Bloomy, I was in the right place at the right time to begin building). Bloomy brings together the different parts of my career that I care most about: educational outcomes, learning design, building products, and getting useful technology into people’s hands.

I’d especially value feedback from parents, teachers, engineers working on child-facing AI, and people who have built tutoring, assessment, or adaptive-learning systems. Does the separation between guided AI help and independent mastery make sense? Where do you see the greatest potential with AI in education? Where are our safeguards insufficient? What evidence or product behavior would you need to trust something like this with a student?

Certainly there are many dangers and pitfalls we must beware of, too, but I believe we can really move the needle in K-12 (for the first time in a long time) if we use AI responsibly and intelligently.

sentences.zh: 我们发现成年人通常不想要另一个通用分数；他们想知道本周哪些少数技能值得关注。Bloomy 通过家庭订阅和学校许可赚钱。

blocks.html_en:
Hi HN, I’m Alex Southmayd, the founder of Bloomy (https://bloomylearning.com) – an AI-powered mastery-learning platform for K-12 students. Bloomy provides students with an AI tutor alongside adaptive curriculum (right now Math, English Language Arts, and Writing).

How it works: we diagnose students’ skill gaps, place them on personalized learning paths, and give them standards-aligned lessons and a Socratic AI tutor that scaffolds their learning without just giving away the answer.

The goal is to solve the Bloom 2-sigma problem (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloom%27s_2_sigma_problem) with AI.

Short launch video: https://tinyurl.com/bloomylearning

Longer product demo: https://youtu.be/XHvoKt6qMeo

Families access for Bloomy: https://bloomylearning.com/families

I started as a teacher. I taught 7th-grade English and writing with Teach For America, and every day I struggled to deliver differentiated instruction to 30 students with 30 different sets of needs. Some students needed remediation, some needed acceleration, and many needed a tutor sitting next to them helping them reason through the next step. Benjamin Bloom’s two-sigma result—that one-on-one tutoring can produce much better outcomes than conventional classroom instruction—always felt intuitively true to me. The hard part was making that kind of attention affordable and available to every child.

Then AI changed the cost curve. When I saw schools such as Alpha organize academics around mastery rather than seat time, the model clicked. If you’ve heard of Alpha School, that is directionally the kind of learning model that inspired us. But I kept thinking about the families and schools that already exist: homeschool families, microschools, hybrid schools, and regular classrooms where most children are today.

Most students and teachers see learning gaps at the wrong resolution. They get a grade, percentile, benchmark score, or broad standard—not “this is the next skill this student should learn.” Existing personalized-learning products often feel like digital worksheets: they provide plenty of practice, but not much diagnosis or teaching. Very few have AI tutors providing the core instruction. Bloomy starts with a diagnostic—we integrate with third-party assessments and provide our own—and creates a learning path for each student. Students work one skill at a time, receive a short lesson, practice at an adaptive difficulty, and only move forward after demonstrating at least 90% mastery. The learning path updates as the student works, based on their performance and our knowledge graph of skill prerequisites (built in collaboration with Learning Commons / Chan Zuckerberg Initiative).

Each skill has three stages. Base Camp teaches the concept with worked examples. Climb provides guided practice and Socratic support. Summit is an independent ten-question mastery assessment with no hints or AI assistance. Students need to achieve 90% on the Summit to advance. If they struggle too much, they’ll be routed to a different skill better suited for their level.

BloomyBot is not a blank chat window but rather a live, interactive, and observant digital tutor. During practice, it receives the active passage or problem, the question, the student’s attempt, an authored explanation, and relevant misconception context. It follows a scaffolded tutoring ladder: first asking what the student tried, then pointing toward the concept, suggesting a strategy, working through one step together, and only providing heavier scaffolding after the student has struggled, adapting to and learning from the student along the way. Students can interrupt it, and we’ve begun to roll out multilingual support for Spanish, French, and a few other more niche languages that customers have asked for.

We currently use a variety of Anthropic and OpenAI models for BloomyBot. The tutor is restricted to the current lesson, redirects unrelated questions, limits conversation length, and is unavailable during mastery assessments. The language model does not choose the curriculum or decide whether a student has mastered a skill.

That separation is important. A conventionally “helpful” AI response can be a bad tutoring response: if it gives away the answer, the student completes the task but may not learn anything. Our goal is not to build a homework-answering chatbot. It is to put AI inside a structured loop of diagnosis, instruction, practice, feedback, and independent mastery.

LLMs can still be wrong, and we do not claim our constraints eliminate that. We reduce the surface area by grounding BloomyBot in authored lesson content, keeping it on topic, logging conversations, and removing it from assessments. Teachers and parents can review tutoring activity, students can report problems, and safety signals trigger human alerts and a backup audit. We also do not see Bloomy as a replacement for teachers, parents, or human tutors. A good human tutor is better. The narrower question we are testing is whether, during a bounded learning session a student would already be doing, a context-aware tutor can provide better help than static “correct/incorrect” feedback. Longer term, the question becomes more whether a student would perform better with one-on-one AI tutoring (at least in certain aspects of the curriculum) than with many-to-one instruction in a medium- or large-sized classroom.

Bloomy is now being used across several settings: traditional districts, charter schools, hybrid schools, microschools, homeschools, and families looking for additional academic support. In an early pilot at a charter school in Massachusetts serving ~150 students in grades 6 through 8, students averaged roughly 1.8 times the expected winter-to-spring NWEA MAP growth. This was an observational pilot, not a randomized study, so we treat it as an encouraging signal rather than proof that Bloomy caused the difference.

Parents and teachers can see what a student has mastered, what is in progress, and where support may be needed. We have found that adults generally do not want another generic score; they want to know which small number of skills deserve attention this week.

Bloomy makes money through family subscriptions and school licensing. ELA costs $39/month or $279/year per learner, and Writing Studio costs $19/month or $139/year. Math is scheduled to launch July 31 at the same price as ELA. Schools and microschools pay per student, with pricing varying by subject coverage, enrollment, rostering, and implementation needs.

Because children use Bloomy, we collect learning responses, progress data, and tutoring conversations. We do not sell personal information, use child data for behavioral advertising, or permit model providers to train general-purpose models on identifiable child data sent by Bloomy. We have Zero Data Retention agreements with both Anthropic and OpenAI. Parents and schools can request access, export, correction, or deletion under the applicable account or school agreement.

More background on me: after Teach For America, I taught and designed GMAT and GRE curriculum for Manhattan Prep / Kaplan, led the driver acquisition team for Lyft’s New England markets, completed an MBA at Stanford, and led AI transformation projects at McKinsey (so when models finally became good enough this past January to achieve the kinds of things I am pursuing with Bloomy, I was in the right place at the right time to begin building). Bloomy brings together the different parts of my career that I care most about: educational outcomes, learning design, building products, and getting useful technology into people’s hands.

I’d especially value feedback from parents, teachers, engineers working on child-facing AI, and people who have built tutoring, assessment, or adaptive-learning systems. Does the separation between guided AI help and independent mastery make sense? Where do you see the greatest potential with AI in education? Where are our safeguards insufficient? What evidence or product behavior would you need to trust something like this with a student?

Certainly there are many dangers and pitfalls we must beware of, too, but I believe we can really move the needle in K-12 (for the first time in a long time) if we use AI responsibly and intelligently.

sentences.zh: 导师仅限于当前课程、重定向不相关的问题、限制对话长度，并且在掌握情况评估期间无法使用。

sentences.en: The language model does not choose the curriculum or decide whether a student has mastered a skill.That separation is important.
sentences.zh: 语言模型不会选择课程或决定学生是否掌握了某项技能。这种分离很重要。

sentences.en: A conventionally “helpful” AI response can be a bad tutoring response: if it gives away the answer, the student completes the task but may not learn anything.
sentences.zh: 传统上“有帮助”的人工智能反应可能是一个糟糕的辅导反应：如果它泄露了答案，学生就完成了任务，但可能不会学到任何东西。

sentences.en: Our goal is not to build a homework-answering chatbot.
sentences.zh: 我们的目标不是构建一个回答作业的聊天机器人。

sentences.en: It is to put AI inside a structured loop of diagnosis, instruction, practice, feedback, and independent mastery.LLMs can still be wrong, and we do not claim our constraints eliminate that.
sentences.zh: 它将人工智能置于诊断、指导、实践、反馈和独立掌握的结构化循环中。法学硕士仍然可能是错误的，我们并不声称我们的限制消除了这一点。

sentences.en: We reduce the surface area by grounding BloomyBot in authored lesson content, keeping it on topic, logging conversations, and removing it from assessments.
sentences.zh: 我们通过将 BloomyBot 融入编写的课程内容、保持主题、记录对话并将其从评估中删除来减少表面积。

sentences.en: Teachers and parents can review tutoring activity, students can report problems, and safety signals trigger human alerts and a backup audit.
sentences.zh: 教师和家长可以审查辅导活动，学生可以报告问题，安全信号会触发人工警报和备份审核。

sentences.en: We also do not see Bloomy as a replacement for teachers, parents, or human tutors.
sentences.zh: 我们也不认为 Bloomy 可以替代老师、家长或人类导师。

sentences.en: A good human tutor is better.
sentences.zh: 一个好的人类导师更好。

sentences.en: The narrower question we are testing is whether, during a bounded learning session a student would already be doing, a context-aware tutor can provide better help than static “correct/incorrect” feedback.
sentences.zh: 我们正在测试的更狭隘的问题是，在学生已经进行的有限学习课程中，情境感知导师是否可以提供比静态“正确/不正确”反馈更好的帮助。

sentences.en: Longer term, the question becomes more whether a student would perform better with one-on-one AI tutoring (at least in certain aspects of the curriculum) than with many-to-one instruction in a medium- or large-sized classroom.Bloomy is now being used across several settings: traditional districts, charter schools, hybrid schools, microschools, homeschools, and families looking for additional academic support.
sentences.zh: 从长远来看，问题变得更多的是，学生在一对一的人工智能辅导（至少在课程的某些方面）是否会比在中型或大型教室中进行多对一教学表现得更好。Bloomy 现在正在多种环境中使用：传统学区、特许学校、混合学校、微型学校、家庭学校和寻求额外学术支持的家庭。

sentences.en: In an early pilot at a charter school in Massachusetts serving ~150 students in grades 6 through 8, students averaged roughly 1.8 times the expected winter-to-spring NWEA MAP growth.
sentences.zh: 在马萨诸塞州一所特许学校的早期试点中，该学校为约 150 名 6 至 8 年级的学生提供服务，学生的平均 MAP 增长速度约为预期冬季至春季 NWEA MAP 增长的 1.8 倍。

sentences.en: This was an observational pilot, not a randomized study, so we treat it as an encouraging signal rather than proof that Bloomy caused the difference.Parents and teachers can see what a student has mastered, what is in progress, and where support may be needed.
sentences.zh: 这是一项观察性试点，而不是一项随机研究，因此我们将其视为一个令人鼓舞的信号，而不是证明 Bloomy 造成了这种差异。家长和老师可以看到学生掌握了什么、正在进步什么以及哪里可能需要支持。

sentences.en: We have found that adults generally do not want another generic score; they want to know which small number of skills deserve attention this week.Bloomy makes money through family subscriptions and school licensing.
sentences.zh: 我们发现成年人通常不想要另一个通用分数；他们想知道本周哪些少数技能值得关注。Bloomy 通过家庭订阅和学校许可赚钱。

sentences.en: ELA costs $39/month or $279/year per learner, and Writing Studio costs $19/month or $139/year.
sentences.zh: ELA 每位学员的费用为 39 美元/月或 279 美元/年，Writing Studio 的费用为 19 美元/月或 139 美元/年。

sentences.en: Math is scheduled to launch July 31 at the same price as ELA.
sentences.zh: Math 计划于 7 月 31 日推出，价格与 ELA 相同。

sentences.en: Schools and microschools pay per student, with pricing varying by subject coverage, enrollment, rostering, and implementation needs.Because children use Bloomy, we collect learning responses, progress data, and tutoring conversations.
sentences.zh: 学校和微型学校按学生付费，价格因学科覆盖范围、入学情况、排班和实施需求而异。由于孩子们使用 Bloomy，我们会收集学习反应、进度数据和辅导对话。

sentences.en: We do not sell personal information, use child data for behavioral advertising, or permit model providers to train general-purpose models on identifiable child data sent by Bloomy.
sentences.zh: 我们不会出售个人信息，不会将儿童数据用于行为广告，也不会允许模型提供商根据 Bloomy 发送的可识别儿童数据来训练通用模型。

sentences.en: We have Zero Data Retention agreements with both Anthropic and OpenAI.
sentences.zh: 我们与 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 签订了零数据保留协议。

sentences.en: Parents and schools can request access, export, correction, or deletion under the applicable account or school agreement.More background on me: after Teach For America, I taught and designed GMAT and GRE curriculum for Manhattan Prep / Kaplan, led the driver acquisition team for Lyft’s New England markets, completed an MBA at Stanford, and led AI transformation projects at McKinsey (so when models finally became good enough this past January to achieve the kinds of things I am pursuing with Bloomy, I was in the right place at the right time to begin building).
sentences.zh: 家长和学校可以根据适用的帐户或学校协议请求访问、导出、更正或删除。更多关于我的背景信息：在 Teach For America 之后，我为曼哈顿预科 / Kaplan 教授和设计了 GMAT 和 GRE 课程，领导了 Lyft 新英格兰市场的司机招募团队，在斯坦福大学完成了 MBA，并领导了麦肯锡的人工智能转型项目（因此，当模型最终在今年 1 月变得足够好以实现我与 Bloomy 追求的目标时，我在正确的时间、正确的地点开始建造）。

sentences.en: Bloomy brings together the different parts of my career that I care most about: educational outcomes, learning design, building products, and getting useful technology into people’s hands.I’d especially value feedback from parents, teachers, engineers working on child-facing AI, and people who have built tutoring, assessment, or adaptive-learning systems.
sentences.zh: Bloomy 汇集了我职业生涯中我最关心的不同部分：教育成果、学习设计、构建产品以及将有用的技术交到人们手中。我特别重视来自家长、教师、致力于面向儿童的人工智能的工程师以及建立辅导、评估或自适应学习系统的人们的反馈。

sentences.en: Does the separation between guided AI help and independent mastery make sense?
sentences.zh: 引导人工智能帮助和独立掌握之间的分离有意义吗？

sentences.en: Where do you see the greatest potential with AI in education?
sentences.zh: 您认为人工智能在教育领域的最大潜力在哪里？

sentences.en: Where are our safeguards insufficient?
sentences.zh: 我们的保障措施哪里还不够？

sentences.en: What evidence or product behavior would you need to trust something like this with a student?Certainly there are many dangers and pitfalls we must beware of, too, but I believe we can really move the needle in K-12 (for the first time in a long time) if we use AI responsibly and intelligently.
sentences.zh: 你需要什么样的证据或产品行为才能让学生相信这样的事情？当然，我们也必须提防许多危险和陷阱，但我相信，如果我们负责任和明智地使用人工智能，我们真的可以在 K-12 中取得进展（很长一段时间以来第一次）。

blocks.html_en: Hi HN, I’m Alex Southmayd, the founder of Bloomy (https://bloomylearning.com) – an AI-powered mastery-learning platform for K-12 students. Bloomy provides students with an AI tutor alongside adaptive curriculum (right now Math, English Language Arts, and Writing).

sentences.en: Hi HN, I’m Alex Southmayd, the founder of Bloomy (https://bloomylearning.com) – an AI-powered mastery-learning platform for K-12 students.
sentences.zh: 大家好，HN，我是 Alex Southmayd，Bloomy (https://bloomylearning.com) 的创始人，这是一个面向 K-12 学生的人工智能掌握学习平台。

sentences.en: Bloomy provides students with an AI tutor alongside adaptive curriculum (right now Math, English Language Arts, and Writing).
sentences.zh: Bloomy 为学生提供人工智能导师以及适应性课程（目前是数学、英语语言艺术和写作）。

blocks.html_en: How it works: we diagnose students’ skill gaps, place them on personalized learning paths, and give them standards-aligned lessons and a Socratic AI tutor that scaffolds their learning without just giving away the answer.

sentences.en: How it works: we diagnose students’ skill gaps, place them on personalized learning paths, and give them standards-aligned lessons and a Socratic AI tutor that scaffolds their learning without just giving away the answer.
sentences.zh: 工作原理：我们诊断学生的技能差距，将他们置于个性化的学习路径上，并为他们提供符合标准的课程和苏格拉底式人工智能导师，在不给出答案的情况下支撑他们的学习。

blocks.html_en: The goal is to solve the Bloom 2-sigma problem (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloom%27s_2_sigma_problem) with AI.

sentences.en: The goal is to solve the Bloom 2-sigma problem (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloom%27s_2_sigma_problem) with AI.
sentences.zh: 目标是用 AI 解决 Bloom 2-sigma 问题 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloom%27s_2_sigma_problem)。

blocks.html_en: I started as a teacher. I taught 7th-grade English and writing with Teach For America, and every day I struggled to deliver differentiated instruction to 30 students with 30 different sets of needs. Some students needed remediation, some needed acceleration, and many needed a tutor sitting next to them helping them reason through the next step. Benjamin Bloom’s two-sigma result—that one-on-one tutoring can produce much better outcomes than conventional classroom instruction—always felt intuitively true to me. The hard part was making that kind of attention affordable and available to every child.

sentences.en: I started as a teacher.
sentences.zh: 我一开始是一名教师。

sentences.en: I taught 7th-grade English and writing with Teach For America, and every day I struggled to deliver differentiated instruction to 30 students with 30 different sets of needs.
sentences.zh: 我在 Teach For America 教 7 年级英语和写作，每天我都努力为 30 名具有 30 种不同需求的学生提供差异化​​的教学。

sentences.en: Some students needed remediation, some needed acceleration, and many needed a tutor sitting next to them helping them reason through the next step.
sentences.zh: 有些学生需要补救，有些学生需要加速，还有许多学生需要坐在他们旁边的导师帮助他们推理下一步。

sentences.en: Benjamin Bloom’s two-sigma result—that one-on-one tutoring can produce much better outcomes than conventional classroom instruction—always felt intuitively true to me.
sentences.zh: 本杰明·布鲁姆的二西格玛结果——一对一辅导可以产生比传统课堂教学更好的结果——对我来说总是直觉上真实的。

sentences.en: The hard part was making that kind of attention affordable and available to every child.
sentences.zh: 困难的部分是让每个孩子都能负担得起并获得这种关注。

blocks.html_en: Then AI changed the cost curve. When I saw schools such as Alpha organize academics around mastery rather than seat time, the model clicked. If you’ve heard of Alpha School, that is directionally the kind of learning model that inspired us. But I kept thinking about the families and schools that already exist: homeschool families, microschools, hybrid schools, and regular classrooms where most children are today.

sentences.en: Then AI changed the cost curve.
sentences.zh: 然后人工智能改变了成本曲线。

sentences.en: When I saw schools such as Alpha organize academics around mastery rather than seat time, the model clicked.
sentences.zh: 当我看到像 Alpha 这样的学校围绕掌握程度而不是座位时间来组织学术时，这个模型就被采纳了。

sentences.en: If you’ve heard of Alpha School, that is directionally the kind of learning model that inspired us.
sentences.zh: 如果您听说过 Alpha School，那么这正是启发我们的定向学习模式。

sentences.en: But I kept thinking about the families and schools that already exist: homeschool families, microschools, hybrid schools, and regular classrooms where most children are today.
sentences.zh: 但我一直在思考已经存在的家庭和学校：家庭学校、微型学校、混合学校以及当今大多数孩子所在的普通教室。

blocks.html_en: Most students and teachers see learning gaps at the wrong resolution. They get a grade, percentile, benchmark score, or broad standard—not “this is the next skill this student should learn.” Existing personalized-learning products often feel like digital worksheets: they provide plenty of practice, but not much diagnosis or teaching. Very few have AI tutors providing the core instruction. Bloomy starts with a diagnostic—we integrate with third-party assessments and provide our own—and creates a learning path for each student. Students work one skill at a time, receive a short lesson, practice at an adaptive difficulty, and only move forward after demonstrating at least 90% mastery. The learning path updates as the student works, based on their performance and our knowledge graph of skill prerequisites (built in collaboration with Learning Commons / Chan Zuckerberg Initiative).

sentences.en: Most students and teachers see learning gaps at the wrong resolution.
sentences.zh: 大多数学生和老师都以错误的方式看待学习差距。

sentences.en: They get a grade, percentile, benchmark score, or broad standard—not “this is the next skill this student should learn.”
sentences.zh: 他们获得成绩、百分位数、基准分数或广泛标准，而不是“这是学生应该学习的下一个技能”。

sentences.en: Existing personalized-learning products often feel like digital worksheets: they provide plenty of practice, but not much diagnosis or teaching.
sentences.zh: 现有的个性化学习产品通常感觉就像数字工作表：它们提供了大量的练习，但没有太多的诊断或教学。

sentences.en: Very few have AI tutors providing the core instruction.
sentences.zh: 很少有人工智能导师提供核心指导。

sentences.en: Bloomy starts with a diagnostic—we integrate with third-party assessments and provide our own—and creates a learning path for each student.
sentences.zh: Bloomy 从诊断开始——我们与第三方评估相结合并提供我们自己的评估——并为每个学生创建一条学习路径。

sentences.en: Students work one skill at a time, receive a short lesson, practice at an adaptive difficulty, and only move forward after demonstrating at least 90% mastery.
sentences.zh: 学生一次学习一项技能，接受简短的课程，以适应性难度进行练习，只有在表现出至少 90% 的掌握程度后才能继续前进。

sentences.en: The learning path updates as the student works, based on their performance and our knowledge graph of skill prerequisites (built in collaboration with Learning Commons / Chan Zuckerberg Initiative).
sentences.zh: 学习路径会随着学生的表现和我们的技能先决条件知识图（与 Learning Commons / Chan Zuckerberg Initiative 合作构建）而更新。

blocks.html_en: Each skill has three stages. Base Camp teaches the concept with worked examples. Climb provides guided practice and Socratic support. Summit is an independent ten-question mastery assessment with no hints or AI assistance. Students need to achieve 90% on the Summit to advance. If they struggle too much, they’ll be routed to a different skill better suited for their level.

sentences.en: Each skill has three stages.
sentences.zh: 每个技能分为三个阶段。

sentences.en: Base Camp teaches the concept with worked examples.
sentences.zh: Base Camp 通过实例教授这一概念。

sentences.en: Climb provides guided practice and Socratic support.
sentences.zh: Climb 提供指导性练习和苏格拉底式支持。

sentences.en: Summit is an independent ten-question mastery assessment with no hints or AI assistance.
sentences.zh: Summit 是一项独立的十个问题掌握程度评估，没有提示或人工智能帮助。

sentences.en: Students need to achieve 90% on the Summit to advance.
sentences.zh: 学生需要在峰会上达到 90% 才能晋级。

sentences.en: If they struggle too much, they’ll be routed to a different skill better suited for their level.
sentences.zh: 如果他们太挣扎，他们就会被引导到更适合他们水平的不同技能。

blocks.html_en: BloomyBot is not a blank chat window but rather a live, interactive, and observant digital tutor. During practice, it receives the active passage or problem, the question, the student’s attempt, an authored explanation, and relevant misconception context. It follows a scaffolded tutoring ladder: first asking what the student tried, then pointing toward the concept, suggesting a strategy, working through one step together, and only providing heavier scaffolding after the student has struggled, adapting to and learning from the student along the way. Students can interrupt it, and we’ve begun to roll out multilingual support for Spanish, French, and a few other more niche languages that customers have asked for.

sentences.zh: BloomyBot 不是一个空白的聊天窗口，而是一个实时、互动且善于观察的数字导师。

sentences.en: During practice, it receives the active passage or problem, the question, the student’s attempt, an authored explanation, and relevant misconception context.

We reduce the surface area by grounding BloomyBot in authored lesson content, keeping it on topic, logging conversations, and removing it from assessments. Teachers and parents can review tutoring activity, students can report problems, and safety signals trigger human alerts and a backup audit. We also do not see Bloomy as a replacement for teachers, parents, or human tutors. A good human tutor is better. sentences.en: The narrower question we are testing is whether, during a bounded learning session a student would already be doing, a context-aware tutor can provide better help than static “correct/incorrect” feedback.
sentences.zh: 我们正在测试的更狭隘的问题是，在学生已经进行的有限学习课程中，情境感知导师是否可以提供比静态“正确/不正确”反馈更好的帮助。

sentences.en: Longer term, the question becomes more whether a student would perform better with one-on-one AI tutoring (at least in certain aspects of the curriculum) than with many-to-one instruction in a medium- or large-sized classroom.
sentences.zh: 从长远来看，问题更多的是，学生在一对一的人工智能辅导（至少在课程的某些方面）是否会比在中型或大型教室中进行多对一教学表现更好。

blocks.html_en: Bloomy is now being used across several settings: traditional districts, charter schools, hybrid schools, microschools, homeschools, and families looking for additional academic support. In an early pilot at a charter school in Massachusetts serving ~150 students in grades 6 through 8, students averaged roughly 1.8 times the expected winter-to-spring NWEA MAP growth. This was an observational pilot, not a randomized study, so we treat it as an encouraging signal rather than proof that Bloomy caused the difference.

blocks.html_en: Parents and teachers can see what a student has mastered, what is in progress, and where support may be needed. We have found that adults generally do not want another generic score; they want to know which small number of skills deserve attention this week.

sentences.en: We have found that adults generally do not want another generic score; they want to know which small number of skills deserve attention this week.
sentences.zh: 我们发现成年人通常不想要另一个通用分数；他们想知道本周哪些少数技能值得关注。

blocks.html_en: Bloomy makes money through family subscriptions and school licensing. ELA costs $39/month or $279/year per learner, and Writing Studio costs $19/month or $139/year. Math is scheduled to launch July 31 at the same price as ELA. Schools and microschools pay per student, with pricing varying by subject coverage, enrollment, rostering, and implementation needs.

blocks.html_en: Because children use Bloomy, we collect learning responses, progress data, and tutoring conversations. We do not sell personal information, use child data for behavioral advertising, or permit model providers to train general-purpose models on identifiable child data sent by Bloomy. We have Zero Data Retention agreements with both Anthropic and OpenAI. Parents and schools can request access, export, correction, or deletion under the applicable account or school agreement.

blocks.html_en: More background on me: after Teach For America, I taught and designed GMAT and GRE curriculum for Manhattan Prep / Kaplan, led the driver acquisition team for Lyft’s New England markets, completed an MBA at Stanford, and led AI transformation projects at McKinsey (so when models finally became good enough this past January to achieve the kinds of things I am pursuing with Bloomy, I was in the right place at the right time to begin building). Bloomy brings together the different parts of my career that I care most about: educational outcomes, learning design, building products, and getting useful technology into people’s hands.

sentences.zh: 更多关于我的背景：在 Teach For America 之后，我为曼哈顿预科 / Kaplan 教授和设计了 GMAT 和 GRE 课程，领导了 Lyft 新英格兰市场的司机招募团队，在斯坦福大学完成了 MBA，并在麦肯锡领导了人工智能转型项目（因此，当模型最终在今年 1 月变得足够好以实现我与 Bloomy 追求的目标时，我在正确的时间、正确的地点开始构建）。

blocks.html_en: Certainly there are many dangers and pitfalls we must beware of, too, but I believe we can really move the needle in K-12 (for the first time in a long time) if we use AI responsibly and intelligently.

content_html_zh: 推出 HN：Bloomy (YC S26) – 由 alexsouthmayd 于 22 小时前进行的 K-1293 点 AI 驱动的掌握学习 |隐藏 |过去|最喜欢 | 98 条评论嗨 HN，我是 Alex Southmayd，Bloomy (https://bloomylearning.com) 的创始人，这是一个面向 K-12 学生的人工智能掌握学习平台。 Bloomy 为学生提供人工智能导师以及适应性课程（目前是数学、英语语言艺术和写作）。它是如何运作的：我们诊断学生的技能差距，将他们置于个性化的学习路径上，并为他们提供符合标准的课程和苏格拉底式人工智能导师，在不给出答案的情况下支撑他们的学习。目标是解决 Bloom 2-sigma 问题 (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloom%27s_2_sigma_problem) AI.Short 启动视频：https://tinyurl.com/bloomylearning 较长的产品演示：https://youtu.be/XHvoKt6qMeoFamilies 访问 Bloomy：https://bloomylearning.com/families 我最初是一名教师。 我在 Teach For America 教 7 年级英语和写作，每天我都努力为 30 名具有 30 种不同需求的学生提供差异化​​的教学。 有些学生需要补救，有些学生需要加速，还有许多学生需要坐在他们旁边的导师帮助他们推理下一步。 本杰明·布鲁姆的二西格玛结果——一对一辅导可以产生比传统课堂教学更好的结果——对我来说总是直觉上真实的。 困难的部分是让每个孩子都能负担得起并获得这种关注。然后人工智能改变了成本曲线。 当我看到像 Alpha 这样的学校围绕掌握程度而不是座位时间来组织学术时，这个模型就被采纳了。 如果您听说过 Alpha School，那么这正是启发我们的定向学习模式。 但我一直在思考已经存在的家庭和学校：在家上学的家庭、微型学校、混合学校以及当今大多数孩子所在的普通教室。大多数学生和老师以错误的方式看待学习差距。 他们获得成绩、百分位数、基准分数或广泛标准，而不是“这是学生应该学习的下一个技能”。 现有的个性化学习产品通常感觉就像数字工作表：它们提供了大量的练习，但没有太多的诊断或教学。 很少有人工智能导师提供核心指导。 Bloomy 从诊断开始——我们与第三方评估相结合并提供我们自己的评估——并为每个学生创建一条学习路径。 学生一次学习一项技能，接受简短的课程，以适应性难度进行练习，只有在表现出至少 90% 的掌握程度后才能继续前进。 学习路径会根据学生的表现和我们的技能先决条件知识图（与 Learning Commons / Chan Zuckerberg Initiative 合作构建）随着学生的工作而更新。每种技能分为三个阶段。 Base Camp 通过实例教授这一概念。 Climb 提供指导性练习和苏格拉底式支持。 Summit 是一项独立的十个问题掌握程度评估，没有提示或人工智能帮助。 学生需要在峰会上达到 90% 才能晋级。 如果他们太挣扎，他们将被引导到更适合他们水平的不同技能。BloomyBot 不是一个空白的聊天窗口，而是一个实时、互动且善于观察的数字导师。 在练习过程中，它接收活跃的段落或问题、问题、学生的尝试、书面解释以及相关的误解背景。 它遵循一个脚手架式的辅导阶梯：首先询问学生尝试了什么，然后指出概念，提出策略，共同完成一个步骤，只有在学生遇到困难后才提供更重的脚手架，一路适应学生并向学生学习。 学生可以打断它，我们已经开始推出对西班牙语、法语和客户要求的其他一些更小众语言的多语言支持。目前，我们为 BloomyBot 使用各种 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 模型。 导师仅限于当前课程、重定向不相关的问题、限制对话长度，并且在掌握情况评估期间无法使用。 语言模型不会选择课程或决定学生是否掌握了某项技能。这种分离很重要。 传统上“有帮助”的人工智能反应可能是一个糟糕的辅导反应：如果它泄露了答案，学生就完成了任务，但可能不会学到任何东西。 我们的目标不是构建一个回答作业的聊天机器人。 它将人工智能置于诊断、指导、实践、反馈和独立掌握的结构化循环中。法学硕士仍然可能是错误的，我们并不声称我们的限制消除了这一点。 我们通过将 BloomyBot 融入编写的课程内容、保持主题、记录对话并将其从评估中删除来减少表面积。 教师和家长可以审查辅导活动，学生可以报告问题，安全信号会触发人工警报和备份审核。 我们也不认为 Bloomy 可以替代老师、家长或人类导师。 一个好的人类导师更好。 我们正在测试的更狭隘的问题是，在学生已经进行的有限学习课程中，情境感知导师是否可以提供比静态“正确/不正确”反馈更好的帮助。 从长远来看，问题变得更多的是，学生在一对一的人工智能辅导（至少在课程的某些方面）是否会比在中型或大型教室中进行多对一教学表现得更好。Bloomy 现在正在多种环境中使用：传统学区、特许学校、混合学校、微型学校、家庭学校和寻求额外学术支持的家庭。 在马萨诸塞州一所特许学校的早期试点中，该学校为约 150 名 6 至 8 年级的学生提供服务，学生的平均 MAP 增长速度约为预期冬季至春季 NWEA MAP 增长的 1.8 倍。 这是一项观察性试点，而不是一项随机研究，因此我们将其视为一个令人鼓舞的信号，而不是证明 Bloomy 造成了这种差异。家长和老师可以看到学生掌握了什么、正在进步什么以及哪里可能需要支持。 我们发现成年人通常不想要另一个通用分数；他们想知道本周哪些少数技能值得关注。Bloomy 通过家庭订阅和学校许可赚钱。 ELA 每位学员的费用为 39 美元/月或 279 美元/年，Writing Studio 的费用为 19 美元/月或 139 美元/年。 Math 计划于 7 月 31 日推出，价格与 ELA 相同。 学校和微型学校按学生付费，价格因学科覆盖范围、入学情况、排班和实施需求而异。由于孩子们使用 Bloomy，我们会收集学习反应、进度数据和辅导对话。 我们不会出售个人信息，不会将儿童数据用于行为广告，也不会允许模型提供商根据 Bloomy 发送的可识别儿童数据来训练通用模型。 我们与 Anthropic 和 OpenAI 签订了零数据保留协议。 家长和学校可以根据适用的帐户或学校协议请求访问、导出、更正或删除。更多关于我的背景信息：在 Teach For America 之后，我为曼哈顿预科 / Kaplan 教授和设计了 GMAT 和 GRE 课程，领导了 Lyft 新英格兰市场的司机招募团队，在斯坦福大学完成了 MBA，并领导了麦肯锡的人工智能转型项目（因此，当模型最终在今年 1 月变得足够好以实现我与 Bloomy 追求的目标时，我在正确的时间、正确的地点开始建造）。 Bloomy 汇集了我职业生涯中我最关心的不同部分：教育成果、学习设计、构建产品以及将有用的技术交到人们手中。我特别重视来自家长、教师、致力于面向儿童的人工智能的工程师以及建立辅导、评估或自适应学习系统的人们的反馈。 引导人工智能帮助和独立掌握之间的分离有意义吗？ 您认为人工智能在教育领域的最大潜力在哪里？ 我们的保障措施哪里还不够？ 你需要什么样的证据或产品行为才能让学生相信这样的事情？当然，我们也必须提防许多危险和陷阱，但我相信，如果我们负责任和明智地使用人工智能，我们真的可以在 K-12 中取得进展（很长一段时间以来第一次）。 帮助
